title: E-Values for Lawyers
primary_topics: three E-values for lawyers (Excellence, Ethics, Eternity)
sub_topics: Emilio C. Capulong Jr. as inducted league president
Dennis Prager 'Happiness Is A Serious Problem' citation
Salonga IBP Rizal Chapter speech ('no lawyer has been chosen president since Ferdinand Marcos')
Many are called but few are chosen (vocation parallel)
Saints are sinners who keep trying (closing aphorism)

stances.claim: The legal profession may be likened to a religious vocation — both demand 'patience, perseverance and prayer' through education, training, sacrifice, and soul-searching to become 'worthy leaders of their faiths.'
stances.rhetorical_move: religious-vocation parallel framing + structured parallelism (law-school = priestly formation)

stances.claim: Excellence in the legal profession 'means more than just knowledge of the law, mastery of legal procedure, and talent in maneuvering courtroom battles' — it involves taking responsibility for consequences, sincerity of intention, and making a difference in others' lives.
stances.rhetorical_move: expanded-definition framing of excellence beyond technical competence

stances.claim: The threefold hierarchy of a lawyer's responsibilities — court/justice first, client second, self last — is often inverted in practice; 'some have come to believe that their interest comes first; next is the interest of their clients; and lastly, if at all, the interest of justice.'
stances.rhetorical_move: doctrinal-hierarchy framing + structured-inversion diagnostic of malpractice

stances.claim: Ethics proceeds from personal integrity — 'to act with integrity is to act with moral courage'; 'only one who is clear of mind and pure of heart can act with integrity.' Transformation requires 'not only a change of philosophy but, more important, also a change of heart.'
stances.rhetorical_move: ethics-as-integrity-as-moral-courage chain framing (echoing Ch 8 Davide tribute)

stances.claim: There is no better yardstick to gauge ethical adherence than one's sense of right and wrong — 'as beings brought into this world by the grace of God, we are inherently possessed of a general discernment of what is good and what is bad'; we remain 'essentially good, God-fearing and just.'

Eternity — the third E — means belief in life after death where final destiny lies; 'just as there are human and physical laws, so are there spiritual laws also' with consequences for transgression; placing God at the center 'will dramatically transform our entire value system.'
stances.rhetorical_move: structural-parallelism (human laws : spiritual laws) + integration framing

stances.claim: God is 'amongst us to embrace us, above us to inspire us, before us to lead us, beside us to guide us, behind us to protect us, and within us — in our hearts and our spirits — to bring us to His Everlasting Kingdom.'
stances.rhetorical_move: enumerative-spatial theology (recurring CJ closing device, used also in column C09)

stances.claim: If saints are sinners who keep trying, lawyers can begin a journey to sainthood — 'as long as we uphold the dignity of the profession, pursue the cause of justice and promote respect for the rule of law, with God at the center of it all, we can be holy in a not-so-holy profession.'

signature_phrases: Many are called, but few are chosen
to act with integrity is to act with moral courage
clear of mind and pure of heart
amongst us to embrace us, above us to inspire us, before us to lead us, beside us to guide us, behind us to protect us, and within us
saints are sinners who keep trying
let us begin our journey to sainthood
a little change can make a big difference
the brave new world of technological advancement, modernization and globalization
register_markers: doctrinal-formal speech register addressing inducted lawyers
Thank you and good day! (formal close)
decision_framework_signals: applies 3-E framework (Excellence / Ethics / Eternity) as lawyer-formation rubric
uses threefold-hierarchy framework (justice/court > client > self) for lawyer-priority test
frames ethics as integrity as moral courage (chain framing echoing the Davide tribute)
uses spatial-theology framing (above/beside/before/behind/within) for divine providence
applies religious-vocation parallel to dignify the legal profession
uses 'saints are sinners who keep trying' aphorism as continuous-improvement ethic
cross_references: expounded extensively in three chapters of his book last year, Transparency, Unanimity & Diversity
notable_anecdotes: Atty. Emilio Capulong Jr. of Bantay Katarungan and Kilosbayan as the inducted Calamba City Lawyers League president — equating his being a lawyer to being 'a servant of the Lord'
Salonga's IBP Rizal speech rueing that 'no lawyer has been chosen president [in our country] since Ferdinand Marcos' as a barometer of public esteem for lawyers
